title: Bridge Paper: The functional role of conscious sensation of movement.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Free will — sense of agency, voluntary action, intentional binding, volitional control.
**Phase context:** sense of agency, voluntary action, free will, intentional binding, volition

Paper: The functional role of conscious sensation of movement.
DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2024.105813
Abstract: This paper proposes a new framework for investigating neural signals sufficient for a conscious sensation of movement and their role in motor control. We focus on signals sufficient for proprioceptive awareness, particularly from muscle spindle activation and from primary motor cortex (M1). Our review of muscle vibration studies reveals that afferent signals alone can induce conscious sensations of movement.
